title: Two Sum
brute_force_explanation: Brute force solution involves iterating through the array to find two elements that add up to the target. This approach has a time complexity of O(n^2) as it needs to compare every element with every other element.
brute_force_steps: [{"description":"Initialize variables","line":1,"state":{}},{"description":"Loop through the array","line":2,"state":{"i":0}},{"description":"Compare current element with remaining elements in the array","line":3,"state":{"i":0,"j":1}},{"description":"Move to next element in the array","line":4,"state":{"i":1,"j":1}},{"description":"Repeat until all elements have been compared","line":5,"state":{}}]
constraints: ["Given an array of integers","And an integer target","Return the indices of two numbers that add up to the target"]
edgeCases: ["Array has less than 2 elements","Target is zero or not present in the array"]
optimal_explanation: The optimal solution uses a hash table to store the numbers in the array and their indices. This allows for constant time lookup of the complement of each number, making the overall time complexity O(n).

problem_statement:
Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

You can return the answer in any order.

 

Example 1:

Input: nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9
Output: [0,1]
Explanation: Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, we return [0, 1].

Example 2:

Input: nums = [3,2,4], target = 6
Output: [1,2]

Example 3:

Input: nums = [3,3], target = 6
Output: [0,1]

 

Constraints:

  • 2 <= nums.length <= 104
  • -109 <= nums[i] <= 109
  • -109 <= target <= 109
  • Only one valid answer exists.

 

Follow-up: Can you come up with an algorithm that is less than O(n2) time complexity?
secondaryPatterns: ["Finding pairs in a sorted dataset","Budget allocation problems","Target weight balancing"]
shortPatternReason: Requires finding a pair with a specific sum.

structuredExamples: [{"input":{"nums":[2,7,11,15],"target":9},"output":[0,1],"explanation":"Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, we return [0, 1]."},{"input":{"nums":[3,2,4],"target":6},"output":[1,2],"explanation":"Because nums[1] + nums[2] == 6, we return [1, 2]."}]
optimal_variants: [{"name":"Hash Table","explanation":"Use a hash table to store the numbers in the array and their indices. Then, iterate through the array and for each number, check if its complement (target - number) is present in the hash table. If it is, return the indices of the two numbers.","complexity":{"time":"O(n)","space":"O(n)"},"steps":[{"description":"Create a hash table","line":1,"state":{}},{"description":"Iterate through the array and store numbers in the hash table","line":2,"state":{"hashTable":{}}},{"description":"For each number in the array, check if its complement is present in the hash table","line":3,"state":{"i":0,"complement":9}},{"description":"Return the indices of the two numbers that add up to the target","line":4,"state":{"x":2,"y":7}}],"pseudocode":"for num in nums: if target - num in hashTable: return [hashTable[target - num], i]"}]
